assertions.expectation: Explains this is related to the interaction between maxSurge, drain order, and pod scheduling
assertions.reason: Response explains the race condition between scheduler placement decisions and node drainage timing, and provides specific maxSurge/maxUnavailable configurations

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE cordons nodes before draining them — pods should prefer scheduling onto uncordoned (upgraded) nodes
assertions.reason: Response doesn't specifically mention GKE's cordoning behavior or explain that pods should naturally prefer uncordoned nodes

assertions.expectation: Identifies possible cause: too many nodes being drained simultaneously (high maxSurge) before enough upgraded nodes exist
assertions.reason: Response identifies parallel/rapid succession upgrades as the root cause and explains nodes being upgraded before stable landing spots exist

assertions.expectation: Recommends reducing maxSurge to 1 to ensure upgraded nodes are available before draining the next batch
assertions.reason: Response specifically shows kubectl patch command setting maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0 configuration

assertions.expectation: Suggests using pod anti-affinity or node affinity to prefer already-upgraded nodes
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed node affinity YAML configuration with preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution to target upgraded nodes

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if any taints or tolerations are causing pods to land on the wrong nodes
assertions.reason: Response mentions using node taints/tolerations for more control over scheduling in the best practices section

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl commands to check node cordoned status and pod scheduling events
assertions.reason: Response includes monitoring script with kubectl commands to check node status and cordoned nodes, plus commands to check pod distribution

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable=0 to ensure old nodes aren't removed until pods are safely rescheduled
assertions.reason: Response explicitly sets maxUnavailable: 0 in the kubectl patch configuration and emphasizes this in best practices

assertions.expectation: May mention GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy as an alternative that avoids this issue by cordoning the entire old pool before migrating workloads to new nodes
assertions.reason: Response shows GKE node pool upgrade commands but doesn't mention the blue-green upgrade strategy or cordoning entire old pools before migration
